perturbation.original_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby .
perturbation.changed_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall be jointly owned by the Disclosing Party and Receiving Party and a perpetual license is granted to the Receiving Party to use said Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing ownership of the 'Confidential Information' from solely the Disclosing Party to joint ownership directly contradicts the original intention. The clause now grants a perpetual license that removes any exclusivity from the Disclosing Party, impacting the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement by changing the ownership and license.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Ownership of Confidential Information . All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall be jointly owned by the Disclosing Party and Receiving Party and a perpetual license is granted to the Receiving Party to use said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text directly contradicts the Original Text and the Contradicted Text. The original text states that 'Confidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party'. The changed text states that they 'shall be jointly owned by the Disclosing Party and Receiving Party and a perpetual license is granted to the Receiving Party to use said Confidential Information.' This change in ownership and the grant of a perpetual license directly contradicts the concept of confidential information belonging solely to the disclosing party and the implied restrictions on use, leading to a clear in-text contradiction. Furthermore, the changed text and contradicted text are exact copies of each other, showing a conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years from the Effective Date, provided that the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall expire five (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is indefinite from the Effective Date, and the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information terminates upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term from a defined two-year period with a five-year confidentiality obligation after disclosure to an indefinite term with immediate termination of confidentiality duties introduces a contradiction. This removes the long-term protection of confidential information and creates a time conflict regarding the confidentiality duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Term. The term of this Agreement is indefinite from the Effective Date, and the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information terminates upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a two-year term with a five-year confidentiality obligation post-disclosure. The changed text alters this to an indefinite term with confidentiality terminating upon agreement expiration. This directly contradicts itself because it removes the specific, longer confidentiality period after the agreement's termination, creating conflicting timelines for confidentiality obligations as described in the explanation. The contradicted text also has the same contradictory language as the Changed text, showing a structural flaw by contradicting the original duration of confidentiality. The new text creates a clear conflict because it creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party as the time frame is not specifically stated.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not liable for any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement and relinquishes any rights of legal action. The Receiving Party does not acknowledge that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, it is agreed that the Disclosing Party shall have NO right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate
perturbation.explanation: The modified text negates the right of the Disclosing Party to seek injunctive relief and states that the Receiving Party is not liable for disclosures of confidential information. This directly contradicts the original clause which provided the Disclosing Party with the right to legal remedies for violations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. The Receiving Party is not liable for any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement and relinquishes any rights of legal action. The Receiving Party does not acknowledge that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, it is agreed that the Disclosing Party shall have NO right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states the Receiving Party is not liable for disclosure and relinquishes legal action rights. The contradicted text repeats this verbatim. The original text provided the Disclosing Party with the right to seek legal remedies. Therefore, the change directly and strongly contradicts the original intent and a prior implicit right, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall immediately return to the Disclosing Part y all written Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and any and all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materials pertaining to such Confidential Information upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party. At the s ame time, the Receiving Party shall delete all copies of such records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not required to return any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, nor delete any records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices, but the Receiving Party will have the option to maintain them for the potential development of mutual and new business opportunities.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text states that the Receiving Party is NOT required to return any confidential information, which completely opposes the previous condition for immediate return and deletion of Confidential Information. Now, there's uncertainty about if information must be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Return of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party is not required to return any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, nor delete any records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices, but the Receiving Party will have the option to maintain them for the potential development of mutual and new business opportunities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the concept of returning or deleting confidential information, as it states the Receiving Party is NOT required to return or delete them. The contradicted text reflects this same changed text, which in itself is a direct contradiction to the original text that stipulated that the receiving party SHALL immediately return confidential information. This creates a clear and impactful in-text contradiction. As the perturbation explanation states, this is a direct contradiction.
